name: Pistol Squat
resistance_profile: Hardest deep in the bottom, where the knee is fully bent and bodyweight leverage on the quads is greatest — strong lengthened loading.
cues: One leg, other leg extended forward
Sit all the way down under control
execution_steps: Stand on one leg with the other extended straight out in front of you.
Hold your arms forward for balance.
Sit all the way down on the working leg under control, keeping the free leg off the floor.
Drive through your whole foot to stand back up without falling backward.
Complete your reps, then switch legs.
good_when: You want an advanced single-leg quad challenge with no equipment.
You're building unilateral strength, balance, and mobility together.
You're progressing bodyweight training now that regular squats are too easy.
bad_when: You lack the mobility or balance yet (regress to box pistols or hold a support).
You need to load the quads heavily for hypertrophy — external load is hard to add.
